excluded_defs.name: setPropertiesBased
excluded_defs.reason: read: already throws for duplicate properties-based constructor registrations, but the issue is that the factory method path skips setPropertiesBased() entirely when skipPropsBased=true, so this guard was never reached

exploration_log.aha_moment: The call to `_addExplicitlyAnnotatedCreators(creators, factories, props, creators.hasPropertiesBased())` passes skipPropsBased=true when a constructor was already found, causing the factory method's setPropertiesBased to be silently skipped. The fix is to throw in the else branch instead of silently skipping.

dead_ends.why_irrelevant: These methods just return annotated members; the ambiguity detection needs to happen where both lists are combined during deserialization setup, which is in POJOPropertiesCollector._collectCreatorProperties()

exploration_log.hindsight: Should have gone directly to POJOPropertiesCollector._collectCreatorProperties() rather than looking at BasicBeanDescription first.

key_decisions.decision: Add else branch in _addExplicitlyAnnotatedCreators() at the exact point where the silent skip happens
key_decisions.reasoning: The else branch is the minimal, most targeted change; PotentialCreators.setPropertiesBased() was already guarded but never reached for the factory method case

search_sequence.action: Search for how constructors and factories are combined in POJOPropertiesCollector

search_sequence.reasoning: Found the skipPropsBased flag and the silent skip path at line 921
search_sequence.result: Confirmed that when skipPropsBased=true the factory method's setPropertiesBased is simply not called, with no warning

search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to understand how to throw from this class without a deserialization context
search_sequence.result: reportProblem() throws IllegalArgumentException with formatted message

queries.query_text: Fix ambiguous @JsonCreator: throw in POJOPropertiesCollector._addExplicitlyAnnotatedCreators when both constructor and factory method have @JsonCreator annotation

solve_notes: The silent skip occurs in `_addExplicitlyAnnotatedCreators` when `skipPropsBased=true` (i.e., a properties-based constructor was already registered). When a properties-based factory method is encountered in this state, the else branch now calls `reportProblem()` instead of silently skipping, producing a clear `IllegalArgumentException` naming the conflicting factory method.
task_text:
When a class has both a `@JsonCreator` annotated static factory method
and a `@JsonCreator` annotated constructor, the introspection logic in
`BasicBeanDescription` silently picks the constructor and ignores the
factory method without warning. Fix the creator detection in
`introspect/` to throw `InvalidDefinitionException` on ambiguous
`@JsonCreator` annotations.

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: exercises _addExplicitlyAnnotatedCreators for classes with explicit @JsonCreator, covering the skipPropsBased path
tests explicit @JsonCreator constructor detection flow through _addExplicitlyAnnotatedCreators

thrash_preventing_defs.name: setPropertiesBased
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: already throws on duplicate propertiesBased registrations, but only when setPropertiesBased is actually called; confirms why the existing guard is insufficient for the factory method skip case

thrash_preventing_defs.name: reportProblem
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: the existing error-reporting helper for this class; an agent must know this method exists before choosing how to signal the error

tier_difference_reasoning: minimum_sufficient has only the edited method. thrash_preventing adds PotentialCreators.setPropertiesBased (an agent would look here first to understand why existing guards don't catch the ambiguous case) and reportProblem (an agent needs to know this helper exists before deciding on IllegalArgumentException vs throwing a custom exception vs using InvalidDefinitionException). Without these, an agent would waste search calls looking for how exceptions are thrown in this class.
